toast.name: Toast
toast.short_description: Cloud-based, restaurant-specific POS platform combining ordering, payments, kitchen display, payroll, and analytics on Android hardware.
toast.starting_price: Free (Starter Kit plan); Point of Sale plan from $69/month plus per-transaction processing fees

features.description: Android-based terminals and handhelds for taking dine-in, takeout, delivery, and online orders through one synced system.
features.title: Point of Sale & Order Management

features.description: Routes tickets from the POS directly to kitchen screens, replacing paper tickets and keeping prep in sync with orders.
features.title: Kitchen Display System

features.description: Commission-free digital ordering for pickup and delivery, integrated with in-house order flow and third-party delivery apps.
features.title: Online Ordering & Delivery

features.description: Built-in loyalty programs, gift cards, and email/SMS marketing campaigns to drive repeat visits.
features.title: Guest Marketing & Loyalty

features.description: Scheduling, payroll processing, and labor law compliance tools centralized alongside POS data.
features.title: Payroll & Team Management

features.description: iOS and Android app for owners and managers to check sales, manage shifts, and toggle ordering channels remotely.
features.title: Toast Now Mobile App

features.description: xtraCHEF-powered tools for tracking food costs, vendor invoices, and stock levels.
features.title: Inventory & Invoice Management

features.description: Centralized Toast Web dashboard with sales, labor, and menu performance reporting across single or multiple locations.
features.title: Reporting & Analytics

features.description: Restaurant financing offers based on a location's own sales history rather than a traditional credit check.
features.title: Toast Capital

features.description: Keeps orders and card payments processing during internet outages, syncing automatically once connectivity returns.
features.title: Offline Mode

pros: Purpose-built for restaurants, with order flow, modifiers, and table management modeled on real food service.
All-in-one platform covering POS, online ordering, kitchen display, payroll, and marketing in a single vendor.
24/7/365 customer support included on every plan, including the free Starter Kit tier.
Fast learning curve reported by front-of-house staff, with new hires often trained within a single shift.
Free Starter Kit tier gives small restaurants and food trucks a low-cost entry point into a full cloud POS.
cons: Proprietary hardware is locked to Toast software and has little resale value if a restaurant switches providers.
Hardware bundles are commonly sold with two-to-three-year commitments that can carry early termination fees.
Paid add-on modules for marketing, digital storefront, and payroll can push real monthly costs well above the base software fee.
Free Starter Kit tier carries higher payment processing rates than the paid Point of Sale plan.
Reviewers report inconsistent customer support experiences and periodic service outages.

faqs.answer: Toast offers a free Starter Kit software tier for one or two terminals at a single location, though it carries higher payment processing rates and restaurants still pay for hardware and onboarding. Paid plans start at $69/month for the Point of Sale tier.
faqs.question: Is Toast POS free to use?

faqs.answer: Yes, Toast has an offline mode that keeps taking orders and card payments during an internet outage and syncs everything once connectivity is restored, though some features like loyalty redemptions and real-time reporting require an active connection.
faqs.question: Does Toast POS work offline?

faqs.answer: No, Toast's point-of-sale terminals and handhelds are proprietary and run only Toast's software, unlike some competitors that work on standard off-the-shelf tablets.
faqs.question: Can I use my own hardware with Toast instead of buying Toast's terminals?

faqs.answer: Toast's POS and kitchen display interfaces support English, Spanish, French, and Chinese, with language set at the device level rather than per individual user.
faqs.question: What languages does the Toast interface support?

faqs.answer: Yes, Toast supports multi-location restaurant groups with centralized reporting, menu management, and labor tracking across all sites from a single Toast Web dashboard.
faqs.question: Can Toast handle multiple restaurant locations?

features: Cloud-based POS for up to two terminals at one location
pricing_plans.price: Free (software); higher per-transaction processing rate applies

features: Guest marketing, loyalty, and gift card programs

streamsets.name: StreamSets
streamsets.short_description: StreamSets (now IBM StreamSets) is a hybrid, multi-cloud platform for building and running streaming and batch data pipelines.

features.description: A library of connectors for databases, applications, and cloud services with a drag-and-drop pipeline designer.
features.title: Pre-built connectors

features.description: An intuitive visual interface for building data pipelines without heavy coding.
features.title: No-code/low-code pipeline builder

features.description: Pipelines automatically adapt to schema changes in source data.
features.title: Automatic data drift handling

features.description: Real-time streaming and scheduled batch processing are supported from the same tool.
features.title: Streaming and batch in one platform

features.description: Deployable on AWS, Azure, Google Cloud, in a private VPC, or on local infrastructure.
features.title: Multi-cloud and hybrid deployment

features.description: A unified interface for monitoring and managing pipelines across environments.
features.title: Central control plane

pros: Handles both streaming and batch data integration in one platform
Automatic data drift handling reduces pipeline maintenance
Broad deployment flexibility across multi-cloud, hybrid, and on-prem environments
Now backed by IBM's enterprise support and ecosystem
cons: Pricing is not publicly listed and requires contacting sales
Enterprise-oriented tool that is likely a heavier lift for very small teams
Now sold and supported as an IBM product following its acquisition

faqs.answer: No, StreamSets (now IBM StreamSets) is a commercial platform priced by contacting IBM sales; no public free tier is listed.
faqs.question: Is StreamSets free to use?

faqs.answer: StreamSets is now part of IBM's product portfolio and is marketed as IBM StreamSets.
faqs.question: Who owns StreamSets today?

faqs.answer: Yes, it supports deployment on AWS, Azure, Google Cloud, in a private VPC, or on local infrastructure.
faqs.question: Can StreamSets run outside the public cloud?

faqs.answer: Yes, it can ingest and transform structured, semi-structured, and unstructured data in both real-time streaming and batch modes.
faqs.question: Does StreamSets support both streaming and batch pipelines?

dropbox-sign.name: Dropbox Sign
dropbox-sign.short_description: Dropbox Sign (formerly HelloSign) is an e-signature platform for sending, signing, and tracking legally binding documents online.

features.description: Upload a document, place signature, initial, date, and text fields, assign them to specific signers, and send the request by email for completion in a browser or mobile device.
features.title: Signature requests

features.description: Save frequently sent documents such as NDAs or standard contracts as templates so fields only need to be mapped once and can be reused for future signers.
features.title: Reusable templates

features.description: A developer API for embedding signature requests, templates, and embedded signing directly into a company's own application or workflow, with webhook support for status updates.
features.title: Dropbox Sign API

features.description: Every completed document includes a tamper-evident audit trail with signer identity details, timestamps, and IP information, plus a certificate of completion.
features.title: Audit trail and certificate of completion

features.description: Collect a signature from someone in the same physical location by handing them a shared device rather than sending an email request.
features.title: In-person signing

features.description: Send the same document to a list of recipients at once, each receiving their own individual copy to sign, useful for onboarding forms or standard agreements.
features.title: Bulk send

features.description: Add a company logo and adjust colors on the signing page so the request looks consistent with a business's own brand, available on Standard and Premium plans.
features.title: Custom branding

features.description: Connects with Dropbox, Google Workspace, Slack, HubSpot, Salesforce, and other business tools to trigger or store signature requests from existing workflows.
features.title: Integrations

features.description: Native iOS and Android apps let senders and signers prepare, send, and complete signature requests from a phone or tablet.
features.title: Mobile apps

pros: Clean, simple interface that most users can pick up without training
Free plan available for people who only need to sign occasionally
Well-documented, developer-friendly API for embedding signatures into other products
Tight integration with Dropbox for teams already storing files there
Generally competitively priced against larger e-signature suites like DocuSign
cons: Free plan is capped at 3 signature requests per month
Standard plan requires a minimum of 2 paid users
Premium plan pricing is not published and requires contacting sales
Fewer advanced contract lifecycle and document-generation features than tools like PandaDoc or DocuSign CLM
Custom branding, bulk send, and deeper integrations are only available on higher-tier plans

faqs.answer: Yes. Dropbox Sign is the rebranded name for HelloSign, an e-signature company founded in 2010 that Dropbox acquired in 2019. Dropbox renamed the product from HelloSign to Dropbox Sign in 2023; the underlying service and account history carried over.
faqs.question: Is Dropbox Sign the same as HelloSign?

faqs.answer: Dropbox Sign offers a free plan that allows up to 3 signature requests per month, with unlimited self-signing. Higher-volume or team use requires upgrading to a paid Essentials, Standard, or Premium plan.
faqs.question: Is Dropbox Sign free to use?

faqs.answer: Yes. The Dropbox Sign API lets developers embed signature requests, templates, and embedded signing directly into their own applications, with webhook support for tracking document status. API access is priced and packaged separately from the standard web app plans.
faqs.question: Does Dropbox Sign have an API?

faqs.answer: Dropbox Sign is designed to produce legally binding electronic signatures under laws such as the U.S. ESIGN Act and UETA, and each completed document includes an audit trail and certificate of completion recording signer details and timestamps. Specific legal requirements can vary by jurisdiction and document type.
faqs.question: Are documents signed with Dropbox Sign legally binding?

faqs.answer: Signers do not need any account to open and sign a document sent through Dropbox Sign. Senders sign up for a separate Dropbox Sign account, which can optionally be connected to an existing Dropbox account for storing and sending files.
faqs.question: Do I need a Dropbox account to use Dropbox Sign?

features: Up to 3 signature requests per month

final-cut-pro.name: Final Cut Pro
final-cut-pro.short_description: Apple's professional video editing software for Mac, featuring the Magnetic Timeline, ProRes support, and Apple Silicon optimization.
final-cut-pro.starting_price: $299.99 one-time purchase (Mac); Final Cut Pro for iPad is a separate subscription

features.description: A trackless, auto-rippling editing model where connected clips shift automatically as you trim or move footage, instead of requiring manual track management.
features.title: Magnetic Timeline

features.description: Synchronizes and lets you switch between multiple camera angles using audio waveforms or timecode, useful for interviews, live events, and music videos.
features.title: Multicam Editing

features.description: Color wheels, curves, and hue/saturation controls with HDR and Dolby Vision support, without needing a separate grading application.
features.title: Built-in Color Grading

features.description: Native decoding and encoding of Apple's professional codecs, with hardware acceleration on Apple Silicon Macs for smoother playback and faster exports.
features.title: ProRes and ProRes RAW Support

features.description: Titles, effects, and transitions created in the companion Motion app, or sourced from third-party designers, that drop directly into the timeline as customizable elements.
features.title: Motion Graphics Templates

features.description: AI-assisted dialogue cleanup, background noise removal, and multichannel audio mixing built directly into the editor.
features.title: Voice Isolation and Audio Tools

features.description: Machine-learning tools that reframe footage for different aspect ratios and track subjects across a shot for effects or captions.
features.title: Smart Conform and Object Tracking

features.description: Rendering, playback, and export performance tuned specifically for the media engines in M-series chips.
features.title: Apple Silicon and Metal Optimization

features.description: A separate subscription app for touch-based, on-location editing that can exchange projects with the Mac version.
features.title: Final Cut Pro for iPad Companion

pros: One-time purchase for the Mac app rather than an ongoing subscription
Magnetic Timeline speeds up assembly and trimming compared with track-based editors
Deep performance optimization for Apple Silicon Macs, including fast ProRes playback and export
Built-in color grading and audio tools reduce the need for separate specialized software
Large third-party ecosystem of plugins, effects, and Motion templates
cons: Mac-only; no Windows or Linux version, and no browser-based editing option
Project files aren't natively compatible with Premiere Pro or Avid Media Composer, complicating cross-platform collaboration
Final Cut Pro for iPad is subscription-based, creating a two-tier pricing model across devices
Fewer centralized asset-management and permission controls for large multi-editor productions compared with some competitors
Editors used to track-based timelines face a real learning curve adapting to the Magnetic Timeline paradigm

faqs.answer: The Mac version of Final Cut Pro is sold as a one-time purchase through the Mac App Store. Final Cut Pro for iPad, a separate app, is subscription-based with monthly or annual billing.
faqs.question: Is Final Cut Pro a subscription or a one-time purchase?

faqs.answer: No. Final Cut Pro is macOS-only; there is no Windows or Linux version, and there is no browser-based edition.
faqs.question: Does Final Cut Pro run on Windows?

faqs.answer: It's Final Cut Pro's trackless editing model, where clips attach to a primary storyline and connected clips automatically shift, or ripple, when you trim or move nearby footage, instead of requiring manual track management.
faqs.question: What is the Magnetic Timeline?

faqs.answer: Yes, Apple offers a free trial of Final Cut Pro for Mac, letting new users test the editing, color, and audio tools before purchasing.
faqs.question: Can I try Final Cut Pro before buying?

faqs.answer: They share editing concepts and can exchange projects, but the Mac version is a one-time purchase aimed at desktop production, while the iPad version is subscription-based and designed around touch input and on-location editing.
faqs.question: What's the difference between Final Cut Pro for Mac and Final Cut Pro for iPad?

features: Built-in color grading and HDR tools
Same feature set as the standard Mac license
Discounted pricing for eligible students and educators
Project compatibility with Final Cut Pro for Mac
Jam feature for collaborative multi-iPad editing

make.name: Make
make.short_description: Make is a visual no-code platform for building, automating, and integrating workflows that connect apps, data, and AI logic.
make.starting_price: Free (paid plans from around $9/month billed annually)

features.description: Build automations as a flowchart on a drag-and-drop canvas, making complex, branching logic easy to follow and edit.
features.title: Visual scenario builder

features.description: Connect popular CRMs, spreadsheets, email tools, e-commerce platforms, and more through pre-built modules.
features.title: Thousands of app integrations

features.description: Use generic HTTP requests, webhooks, and custom API calls to integrate services that don't have a native connector.
features.title: Custom API and webhook modules

features.description: Split a single scenario into multiple conditional paths so different data can trigger different downstream actions.
features.title: Routers and branching logic

features.description: Iterators, aggregators, and built-in functions reshape, filter, and combine data as it flows between apps.
features.title: Data mapping and transformation tools

features.description: Attach error handlers, retry logic, and fallback routes to scenarios so failures don't silently break a workflow.
features.title: Error handling and retries

features.description: Start from a library of pre-built templates for common automation patterns instead of building from scratch.
features.title: Scenario templates

features.description: Inspect past runs and data bundles at each step to debug issues and understand exactly what happened in a scenario.
features.title: Execution logs and history

features.description: Incorporate large language model steps and AI agent building blocks directly into automation workflows.
features.title: AI modules and agents

features.description: Run scenarios on a schedule or trigger them instantly via webhooks for near real-time automation.
features.title: Scheduling and instant triggers

pros: Visual, flowchart-style builder makes complex multi-branch automations easier to understand than list-based tools
Very wide reach through native integrations plus generic HTTP and webhook modules for almost any API
Strong data mapping and transformation tools for reshaping information between apps
Free plan and template library make it accessible to try before committing to a paid plan
Detailed execution logs and error-handling options support serious debugging and reliability
cons: Steeper learning curve than simpler, wizard-driven automation tools for first-time users
Usage-based operations/credits pricing can make costs less predictable for high-volume workflows
Some advanced concepts (bundles, iterators, aggregators) require a bit of trial and error to master
Very deep, vendor-certified integrations for specific enterprise systems may be thinner than dedicated point solutions
Complex scenarios with many modules can become visually cluttered and harder to navigate on the canvas

faqs.answer: Yes, Make offers a free plan with a limited monthly allowance of operations/credits, which is enough for light automation use. Paid plans remove those limits and add features like higher execution priority and more credits.
faqs.question: Is Make free to use?

faqs.answer: Make was previously known as Integromat. It was founded in 2012, acquired by Celonis in 2020, and rebranded as Make in 2022 as part of a broader repositioning of the product.
faqs.question: What was Make called before?

faqs.answer: No, Make is designed as a no-code visual builder, so most automations can be created by dragging and connecting modules. That said, comfort with concepts like JSON and APIs helps when working with custom HTTP requests or advanced data mapping.
faqs.question: Do I need to know how to code to use Make?

faqs.answer: Both are automation platforms, but Make emphasizes a visual, flowchart-style canvas that shows branching logic and data flow directly, while Zapier traditionally favors a simpler, more linear step-by-step setup.
faqs.question: How is Make different from Zapier?

faqs.answer: Yes, Make is built for scenarios with routers, loops, error handlers, and multiple branching paths, which makes it well suited to more elaborate workflows beyond simple one-to-one app connections.
faqs.question: Can Make handle complex, multi-step business processes?

features: Access to the visual scenario builder
Access to thousands of app integrations
Multi-user access with team roles and permissions
Shared scenario templates across a team
pricing_plans.price: From around $29/month (Teams); custom pricing for Enterprise

odoo-point-of-sale.name: Odoo Point of Sale
odoo-point-of-sale.short_description: Odoo Point of Sale is a cloud and offline-capable POS app that syncs sales, inventory, and accounting across Odoo's business suite.
odoo-point-of-sale.starting_price: Free for one app (limited users); paid plans start around $24.90/user/month billed annually

features.description: The register keeps operating without an internet connection, storing transactions locally and syncing them automatically once connectivity is restored.
features.title: Offline mode

features.description: Every sale updates stock levels immediately when connected to Odoo Inventory, reducing manual stock counts and overselling.
features.title: Real-time inventory sync

features.description: Centralized reporting and shared product catalogs let retailers manage several locations and registers from one back office.
features.title: Multi-store and multi-register support

features.description: Configurable floor plans, course sequencing, and kitchen order routing support full-service restaurants and cafes.
features.title: Restaurant floor and table management

features.description: Built-in connections to several major card payment providers speed up checkout and reduce manual reconciliation.
features.title: Payment terminal integrations

features.description: Works with barcode scanners, receipt printers, cash drawers, and scales for a standard retail hardware setup.
features.title: Barcode scanning and hardware support

features.description: Configurable loyalty points, gift cards, and discount rules encourage repeat purchases directly from checkout.
features.title: Loyalty programs and coupons

features.description: Staff PIN codes and role-based permissions control access to discounts, refunds, and register functions.
features.title: Employee login and permissions

features.description: Sales data flows into Accounting, CRM, and eCommerce modules, keeping financials and customer records consistent.
features.title: Unified back office with Odoo apps

features.description: Receipt templates and built-in sales dashboards can be adjusted to match branding and reporting needs.
features.title: Custom receipts and reporting

pros: Deep integration with Odoo Inventory, Accounting, and CRM keeps data consistent across the business
Offline mode allows uninterrupted sales during connectivity issues
Highly configurable to fit both retail and restaurant workflows
Free single-app tier lowers the barrier for small operators to try it
Scales into a full business suite as a company adds more Odoo apps
cons: Initial setup and configuration can be time-consuming compared to simpler standalone POS apps
Full value depends on adopting other Odoo modules, which adds cost and complexity
Support quality can vary depending on whether it's handled directly or through a partner
Interface and workflow changes roll out with the wider Odoo platform, requiring periodic adjustment
Advanced hardware or niche vertical features may lag behind dedicated single-purpose POS products

faqs.answer: Odoo offers a free plan limited to one app, which can include Point of Sale for a small number of users. Businesses needing multiple apps or more users move to paid Standard or Custom plans.
faqs.question: Is Odoo Point of Sale free to use?

faqs.answer: Yes, it includes an offline mode that lets staff continue processing sales during connectivity interruptions, syncing all transactions once the connection is back.
faqs.question: Does Odoo Point of Sale work without internet access?

faqs.answer: Yes, it includes restaurant-specific features such as floor plans, table management, and kitchen order routing, alongside retail features like barcode scanning and multi-store inventory.
faqs.question: Can Odoo Point of Sale be used for restaurants as well as retail?

faqs.answer: No, it can run as a standalone app, but connecting it to Odoo Inventory, Accounting, or CRM unlocks automatic stock updates, financial syncing, and customer tracking.
faqs.question: Do I need other Odoo apps to use Point of Sale?

faqs.answer: It supports common POS hardware including barcode scanners, receipt printers, cash drawers, scales, and integrations with several major payment terminal providers.
faqs.question: What hardware does Odoo Point of Sale support?

features: One Odoo app of your choice, such as Point of Sale
Full access to Point of Sale features
Access to additional Odoo apps as needed
Suited for larger or multi-location businesses

States compliance with ESIGN Act, UETA, 21 CFR Part 11, SOC2, and HIPAA
cons: Free plan is limited to 5 documents per month and 10 recipients per document
White-label and enterprise add-ons require the higher-priced Platform or Enterprise tier
Language support is not documented on the website
Company headquarters and founding year are not disclosed on the site

faqs.answer: Yes, Documenso states its code is open source and available on GitHub, and it can be self-hosted via Docker.
faqs.question: Is Documenso open source?

faqs.answer: Yes, the Free plan allows up to 5 documents per month with up to 10 recipients per document, and no credit card is required.

faqs.answer: Yes, Documenso offers a self-hosted licensing option in addition to its hosted cloud service.

faqs.answer: Documenso states support for the ESIGN Act, UETA, 21 CFR Part 11, SOC2, and HIPAA.
faqs.question: What compliance standards does Documenso support?

faqs.answer: Yes, API access is included starting with the Individual plan, with unlimited API access on the Platform plan.
faqs.question: Does Documenso offer an API?

sevenrooms.name: SevenRooms

sevenrooms.headquarters: New York City, New York, United States

pros: Distributes reservations through DoorDash and Deliveroo in addition to Google, Instagram, and Yelp
AI table management and voice AI aimed at reducing missed reservations
Serves a wide range of venue types beyond restaurants, including hotels, clubs, and wineries
Used by over 15,000 restaurants globally, per the company
cons: No public pricing; requires booking a demo to get a quote
Now part of DoorDash following its 2025 acquisition, which could shift product priorities
Enterprise-oriented feature set may be more than small independent restaurants need

faqs.answer: SevenRooms is owned by DoorDash, which completed its acquisition of the company in June 2025.
faqs.question: Who owns SevenRooms?

faqs.answer: SevenRooms serves restaurants, restaurant groups, hotels, membership clubs, nightclubs and bars, sports and entertainment venues, and breweries and wineries.

squadcast.name: SquadCast
squadcast.short_description: Cloud-based remote recording studio with progressive uploads, multi-track editing, and AI-assisted post-production.

features.description: Proprietary technology auto-saves recordings during the session to protect against connectivity loss.
features.title: Progressive Uploads

features.description: Captures each participant's audio and video separately to avoid drift and sync issues.
features.title: Separate audio/video recording

features.description: Adjust individual tracks for cleanup, processing, and volume correction.
features.title: Multi-track editing

features.description: Keeps multiple backup copies of recordings to protect against data loss.
features.title: Cloud backup

features.description: Guests join sessions in the browser with no downloads or installation required.
features.title: In-browser interface

features.description: Includes AI features such as transcription and AI speech tools, with usage limits scaling by plan.
features.title: AI suite

features.description: Connects with tools including Zapier, Dolby, Dropbox, and Descript.
features.title: Integrations

pros: Free plan available to try the platform
Progressive Uploads protect recordings from connectivity failures during a session
Separate audio/video tracks per participant reduce sync issues
Used by organizations such as Google, Spotify, NPR, Microsoft, and ESPN per the company's site
In-browser guest experience requires no software installation
cons: Free plan limited to 1 recording hour per editor per month and exports with watermarks
AI features are capped by monthly usage limits that scale with plan tier
Higher recording-hour and team-size needs require the more expensive Business plan

faqs.answer: Yes, SquadCast has a free plan offering 1 recording hour per editor per month, 1 show, and exports up to 720p with watermarks.
faqs.question: Is SquadCast free?

faqs.answer: It's SquadCast's proprietary technology that auto-saves recordings during a session, protecting against connectivity loss or technical failures.
faqs.question: What does Progressive Uploads do?

faqs.answer: Yes, it records each participant's audio and video separately, which the company says eliminates drift and sync issues common in remote recording.
faqs.question: Does SquadCast separate audio and video tracks?

faqs.answer: The Hobbyist plan ($16/month billed annually or $24/month billed monthly) includes 10 recording hours/editor/month, 5 shows, unlimited watermark-free exports up to 1080p, and a basic AI suite.
faqs.question: What's included in the Hobbyist plan?

faqs.answer: No, SquadCast runs in the browser, so guests can join without downloads or installation.
faqs.question: Do guests need to install software to join a SquadCast session?

coolify.name: Coolify
coolify.short_description: Coolify is a free, open-source, self-hostable alternative to Heroku and Vercel for deploying apps to your own servers.

features.description: Deploy to any server you own, including VPS, Raspberry Pi, or cloud providers, with no vendor lock-in.
features.title: Self-hosted deployment

features.description: Deploy databases and popular services with one click, alongside apps in multiple programming languages.
features.title: 280+ one-click services

features.description: Automatic database backups and free SSL certificate management via Let's Encrypt.
features.title: Automatic backups and SSL

features.description: Connects with GitHub, GitLab, Bitbucket, and Gitea for deployment workflows.
features.title: Git integration

features.description: Real-time terminal access to servers directly from the browser.
features.title: Browser-based terminal

features.description: Tools for teams to collaborate on deployments and infrastructure.
features.title: Team collaboration

pros: Self-hosting is free forever and fully open source
No vendor lock-in — deploy to any server you own
Supports 280+ one-click services
Low-cost managed Coolify Cloud tier starting at $5/month
Supports multiple Git providers (GitHub, GitLab, Bitbucket, Gitea)
cons: Self-hosted setup requires managing your own server infrastructure
Coolify Cloud still requires bringing your own servers to deploy to
Only community and limited email support on the base Cloud plan

faqs.answer: Yes, self-hosting Coolify is free forever and open source.
faqs.question: Is Coolify free?

faqs.answer: A managed control panel service starting at $5/month for 2 connected servers, with $3/month per additional server.
faqs.question: What is Coolify Cloud?

faqs.answer: Yes, both the self-hosted and Cloud versions require you to bring your own servers, such as a VPS or cloud instance.
faqs.question: Do I need my own server to use Coolify?

faqs.answer: Over 280 one-click services plus custom applications in multiple programming languages.
faqs.question: What can I deploy with Coolify?

dify.name: Dify
dify.short_description: Dify is an open-source platform for visually building, testing, and deploying AI agents, workflows, and RAG pipelines.

features.description: Visual, node-based builder for creating agentic workflows without managing infrastructure.
features.title: Workflow Studio

features.description: Configure agents with memory, tools, and defined boundaries.
features.title: Agent building

features.description: Document processing and indexing to build retrieval-augmented generation (RAG) knowledge bases.
features.title: Knowledge pipeline

features.description: Access to model providers, tools, data sources, and MCP-compatible integrations.
features.title: Marketplace

features.description: Deploy apps as web apps, REST APIs, embeds, or MCP-compatible tools.
features.title: Multiple publishing options

features.description: Logs, user feedback, latency, and usage tracking for deployed apps.
features.title: Monitoring

features.description: Framework for building custom tools, models, and integrations, plus a difyctl CLI for CI/CD workflows.
features.title: Plugin development

pros: Open-source Community Edition can be self-hosted via Docker Compose at no cost
149K+ GitHub stars and an active open-source community
Free Dify Cloud Sandbox tier available with no installation required
Deployment flexibility: managed cloud, self-hosted, or private/VPC Enterprise deployment
Enterprise tier offers SOC 2 Type II and ISO 27001 compliance plus SSO/SAML and RBAC
cons: Source code is released under a modified Apache 2.0 license that requires a commercial license for multi-tenant SaaS use
Self-hosting the Community Edition requires Docker and technical setup
Paid Cloud plans (Professional, Team) are billed annually, with no listed monthly option
Enterprise pricing is custom and not publicly listed

faqs.answer: Yes. The Community Edition is open source and free to self-host, and Dify Cloud offers a free Sandbox tier with 200 message credits and limited usage.
faqs.question: Is Dify free to use?

faqs.answer: Yes, the open-source Community Edition can be self-hosted with Docker Compose, and an Enterprise self-hosted option with VPC deployment is also available.
faqs.question: Can Dify be self-hosted?

faqs.answer: Dify is released under a modified version of the Apache License 2.0 that permits commercial use but requires a commercial license for offering Dify as a multi-tenant SaaS service.
faqs.question: What license does Dify use?

faqs.answer: Dify Cloud's Professional plan starts at $590/year and the Team plan is $1,590/year; a free Sandbox tier and custom Enterprise pricing are also available.
faqs.question: How much does Dify Cloud cost?

faqs.answer: Dify is developed by LangGenius, headquartered in Menlo Park, California, with additional teams in Tokyo, Suzhou, and Shanghai.
faqs.question: Who makes Dify?

features: All core features under the public repository
SOC 2 Type II and ISO 27001 compliance
